pattern: Sorting box capacities in descending order lets you greedily pick the largest boxes until cumulative capacity covers all apples.
hints: You only care about total apple count, not which pack they came from. Compute the sum first.
To minimize the number of boxes, always prefer the largest remaining box. Sort capacities in descending order and count how many you need.
Accumulate sorted capacities from largest to smallest; stop as soon as the running total meets or exceeds the total number of apples.

edge_inputs.description: single pack, single box that fits exactly
one apple total, smallest capacity boxes
large capacity box available but answer uses fewer large boxes not many small ones
capacities in ascending order - must sort to get minimum count
maximum size: 50 packs of 50 apples, all boxes capacity 50

stress.description: random apples and capacities up to size 50
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(size, 50)
    m = min(size, 50)
    apple = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    total = sum(apple)
    # guarantee solvable: add a box with capacity = total
    capacity = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(m - 1)] + [total]
    rng.shuffle(capacity)
    return [apple, capacity]
